name: From Ecija to Puente Genil – circular
name_original: Desde Écija a Puente Genil – circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Écija traverse a landscape characterized by fertile agricultural heartlands, including extensive cereal fields, olive groves, and orange orchards in the Santa Lucía Valley. The region's flat to gently rolling terrain is shaped by the Genil River and its fluvial terraces, providing a rural backdrop for cycling. Many routes utilize repurposed infrastructure, such as segments of old railway lines like the Vía Verde de la Campiña, offering well-established paths through the countryside. This diverse setting provides varied road cycling terrain suitable for different preferences. Best road cycling routes around Écija The most popular road cycling route is From Ecija to Puente Genil – circular, a 50.5 miles (81.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate ride through the agricultural landscapes surrounding Écija. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Écija and La Campiña Countryside Loop, a moderate 52.0 miles (83.7 km) path. It winds through the open fields and rural scenery of the Campiña region. Local road cyclists also love the Peñaflor Palace – Marinaleda loop from Écija, a 71.9 miles (115.8 km) trail leading through agricultural heartlands and past historic towns, often completed in about 5 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Écija is defined by open agricultural fields, rural countryside, and routes along old railway lines. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Écija are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Écija's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Écija?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Écija, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes traverse the region's agricultural heartlands and rural landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Écija?
A: The terrain around Écija is characterized by fertile agricultural heartlands, including extensive cereal fields, olive groves, and orange orchards in the Santa Lucía Valley. You'll find mostly flat to gently rolling landscapes, shaped by the Genil River and its fluvial terraces, providing a scenic rural backdrop for your rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Écija?
A: Due to Écija's famously high summer temperatures, the best times for road cycling are typically during the milder seasons of &lt;strong&gt;spring&lt;/strong&gt; (March to May) or &lt;strong&gt;autumn&lt;/strong&gt; (September to November). These periods offer more comfortable weather conditions for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for different skill levels in Écija?
A: Yes, the routes around Écija offer options for various skill levels. You can find easy, moderate, and difficult routes. There are 3 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for everyone from beginners to experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes from Écija?
A: Yes, many routes around Écija are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994374316/from-ecija-to-puente-genil-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Ecija to Puente Genil – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate ride through agricultural landscapes.

Q: What historical or natural sights can I see along road cycling routes in Écija?
A: While cycling, you can encounter Écija's rich history and architectural beauty, often referred to as the 'City of Towers.' Notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6429603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España (El Salón)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Santa María and Santa Bárbara&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also pass through natural features like the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4352235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía valley&lt;/a&gt; with its orange groves and the general &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2884260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Écija countryside&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Écija?
A: The road cycling routes in Écija are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the open agricultural fields, rural countryside, and the well-established paths along old railway lines.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the region around Écija utilizes repurposed infrastructure for cycling. Segments of old railway lines, such as the 'Vía Verde de la Campiña' and the 'Camino Natural de la Campiña Sevillana,' offer well-established and scenic paths through the countryside.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling options near Écija?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Palace – Marinaleda loop from Écija&lt;/a&gt; cover over 70 miles (115 km), leading through agricultural heartlands and past historic towns. Other routes also connect Écija to nearby towns like Puente Genil and Palma del Río.

Q: What towns can I cycle to from Écija?
A: From Écija, you can cycle to several nearby towns and explore the surrounding region. Popular destinations include Puente Genil, Marinaleda, Peñaflor, and Palma del Río, each offering unique sights and experiences. For example, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Benamejí – Palma del Río loop from Écija&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several viewpoints offer scenic vistas along the routes. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4488840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Almodóvar del Río from CO-3313&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Almodóvar del Río and its castle&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque breaks during your ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Écija?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit for road cycling, the presence of 3 easy routes and the generally flat to gently rolling terrain, especially along repurposed railway lines, suggests that some routes could be suitable for families with older children or those looking for less challenging rides.
tips: Écija was the town that won the Diario de Sevilla contest for the most beautiful town in the province of Seville. Also called "the City of Towers" for its famous 11 towers and "the City of the Sun" referring to its flag.

The name of this pretty town comes from the river Genil, which runs through this town. Puente Genil has several examples of civil architecture, among them: Stewardship of the Dukes of Medinaceli, Teatro Circo, Casa Consistorial.
The Palacio is home to a museum. Above all, the finds from the Moorish and Roman periods that were discovered during excavations in the Plaza d'España can be seen here.

waypoints.name: Municipal Archaeological Museum of Puente Genil
